text.tspan: Scaling the OLMo insecure-code adapter amplifies its self-report of insecure code
Take each trained insecure-code adapter (250 / 500 / 750 / 1000 training examples), multiply its LoRA delta by α, and re-run the probes.
Organism: Olmo-3-7B-Instruct. The prereg (docs/prereg_olmo_alpha_scaling.md) predicted this self-report channel stays flat under scaling.

svg.text: The organism's own self-report of writing insecure code
trustworthy channel — its forced A/B choice between “The code I
write is often insecure…” and “…generally secure…”, both orders;
the four dose lines lie on top of each other:
dose does not move it — scaling α does
at α = 1 — the adapter as trained — every
dose sits at the 0.25 base. The prereg
“mirror” claim said it stays flat under
scaling. Instead it rises at every dose:
α — multiplier on the adapter's LoRA delta
(α = 1 is the adapter as trained; citable window α ≤ 1.5)
on free generations · same 0–0.5 scale as left
miscalibrated on OLMo — not usable as evidence
α — multiplier on the adapter's LoRA delta
every other dose behaves the same way: em_choice rises
+0.11 to +0.18 over α 0.5 → 1.5 at each dose; em_freegen
Reading: the insecure-code direction is already encoded in the adapter but under-expressed at its trained scale. At α = 1 every dose
sits at the 0.25 base (the dose ladder moved self-report by at most 0.04); linearly scaling the same delta expresses it, +0.16 to
+0.20 by α = 1.5. The preregistered mirror hypothesis — OLMo carries behavior, not self-report, the inverse of Qwen — is retired: on
this channel OLMo behaves like Qwen. No free-generation behavioral claim survives (the only such channel is a miscalibrated judge),
and α = 2 fails the off-target gate (agreeableness 0.10–0.12 at α = 1 rising to 0.43–0.62 at α = 2), leaving α ≤ 1.5 as the citable
Report: docs/report_olmo_alpha_scaling.md · em_freegen flag: docs/report_em_freegen_manual_adjudication.md · Qwen companion figure: methods_alpha_scaling.svg
